title: glycine oblongifolia
intro: Glycine oblongifolia, also known as Sweet Gale or Chinese Silver Fir, is a type of evergreen tree native to China and Japan, characterized by its distinctive, aromatic leaves and small, white flowers. Traditionally used in Chinese medicine, Glycine oblongifolia is valued for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Glycine oblongifolia include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and reduce fever. Glycine oblongifolia can be prepared into various herbal forms, including tea, decoctions, and infusions, which are often used to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and improve overall well-being.
